cluster_name: Turkish police storm CHP headquarters, opposition leadership ousted
tldr: The headline layer across 20 outlets presents a strikingly uniform front: riot police storming CHP headquarters and the political crisis it embodies — there is virtually no disagreement on what the story is. Germany's Süddeutsche Zeitung is the sole outlet whose headline foregrounds the democracy-collapse interpretation openly ('How do you save a democracy that is no longer one?'), a contrast with Anglophone wire coverage that sticks to factual event description. Breitbart is the only outlet to specify the weapons used ('Firing Tear Gas and Rubber Bullets'), while The New Arab is the only outlet to headline a related but distinct story — the detention of 13 CHP congress delegates. The Bianet English headline presenting the Turkish justice minister's counter-framing ('reinforced trust in Turkey’s democracy') offers the only pro-government voice in the headline corpus, illustrating how Turkey's domestic media framed the same event as a democratic success.
event_summary: On May 25, 2026, Turkish riot police stormed CHP party headquarters in Ankara using tear gas and rubber bullets to evict opposition leader Ozgur Ozel, following a court ruling that annulled his 2023 election as party chairman and reinstated predecessor Kemal Kilicdaroglu.

evidence.quote: Turkish police storm CHP headquarters amid political crisis
opposition out of headquarters as crisis deepens
Türkei: Wie rettet man eine Demokratie, die keine mehr ist?

frames.sub_frame: Court ouster of CHP leadership contested

evidence.quote: CHP Challenges Court's Ousting of Party Leadership
destituido Ozgur Özel en el CHP
Firing Tear Gas and Rubber Bullets
Turkey detains 13 over congress that chose ousted leader

bottom_line: Headline-layer coverage is near-uniform across 20 outlets in 14 countries — riot police storming CHP headquarters framed as a political crisis or democracy test — but the sharpest divergences are Germany's Süddeutsche Zeitung posing an openly editorial question about Turkey's democratic survival, Breitbart uniquely foregrounding police weapons, and The New Arab being the sole outlet to headline the parallel arrest of 13 CHP congress delegates rather than the headquarters raid itself.
